title: Public Health Engineering (2015602) PYQ
description: Previous Year Questions for Civil Engineering (Theory) - Public Health Engineering covering water treatment, sanitation, wastewater management, plumbing systems, and environmental engineering concepts.

questions.question: Water pollution can be caused by excess __________ from industries and __________ from agricultural runoff.
Which sanitary fitting is specifically designed for urination?
In the activated sludge process, which of the following is used to facilitate the growth of microorganisms?
What is the primary purpose of a sedimentation tank in wastewater treatment?
options: To remove solid wastes through settling

questions.question: In solid waste management, the main function of an __________ is to reduce volume, while a __________ helps to separate recyclables.
The process of filtration is used to remove suspended particles from water in a wastewater treatment plant.
Are landfills considered the most sustainable method of managing solid waste in the long term?
Which process is commonly used as a disinfection method in drinking water treatment?
What is the primary function of a trap in a building's plumbing system?
options: To increase the flow of water
To retain water and prevent foul gases from entering the building

questions.question: Composting is an __________ process that converts organic waste into soil amendments.
What is the chemical name of bleaching powder?
When is double chlorination typically adopted in water treatment?
options: When the water is exceptionally clear
When there is an epidemic in the locality

questions.question: Which of the following is another name for a Trickling Filter?
A septic tank functions as which of the following?
Which of the following water quality parameters is NOT correctly matched with its determination method?
According to Kuichling’s formula, the fire demand of water (Q) in liters/minute is given by:
Aerobic reactions take place in the presence of __________ and produce stable __________ end products.
Which statement is true regarding the drainage of buildings?
options: One pipe system is cheaper than the single stack system
Single stack system is generally more economical than the one pipe system
Both systems cost exactly the same
One pipe system is only used for industrial waste

questions.question: In a septic tank, the outlet pipe is typically kept about _________ lower than the inlet pipe.
Match the pollutants with their permissible limits in the absence of alternate sources.
options: Chloride-1000 mg/l, Sulphate-400 mg/l, Total Alkalinity-600 mg/l
Chloride-400 mg/l, Sulphate-1000 mg/l, Total Alkalinity-600 mg/l
Chloride-600 mg/l, Sulphate-400 mg/l, Total Alkalinity-1000 mg/l
Chloride-1000 mg/l, Sulphate-600 mg/l, Total Alkalinity-400 mg/l
